formations_blocks: Sweden pushed back into a deeper defensive block
adjustments: Dutch press and offside line used early to pin Sweden back
Midfielders bursting on from deep, with De Jong pulling strings from deep
phases.summary: The Netherlands started on the front foot, using a high press and aggressive vertical runs to trap Sweden deep and force hurried clearances. Their early energy paid off with Brobbey’s sixth-minute goal, while Sweden’s best responses came through Gyokeres and an attempted set-piece routine, but they were largely pinned back by Dutch intensity and the threat from Dumfries and Malen.

formations_blocks: Netherlands high-tempo attacking shape, repeatedly getting men into the Sweden box
Sweden initially under pressure in a disorganized defensive shape
Sweden's planned switch to a back four with Gudmundsson moving into the back line
adjustments: Sweden’s Potter plan to change to a back four, with Gudmundsson into the four
Netherlands continuing to target runs into the Sweden box rather than settling into possession
key_matchups: Gyokeres vs the Netherlands flank/defensive line
phases.summary: The phase is dominated by Dutch pressure, with Reijnders making repeated bursts into the box and Brobbey scoring to leave Sweden scrambling. Sweden look badly stretched and, in response, Potter’s side prepare a shift to a back four, which briefly creates a route for Gyokeres to escape down the flank and force a half-chance. The overall tactical story is one of Netherlands control and Sweden trying to re-balance their shape after being pulled in multiple directions.

adjustments: Isak’s movement is dragging defenders away from Gyokeres, creating space between the two forwards
phases.summary: The main tactical note is Isak’s intelligent movement, which repeatedly pulls defenders away from Gyokeres and helps Sweden create a better chance on goal. The sequence ends with Gyokeres forcing a save from Verbruggen, suggesting Sweden were improving just before half-time despite the scoreline of 2-0 to the Netherlands.

formations_blocks: Sweden deep defensive block under pressure
Sweden counterattacking moments after the restart
adjustments: Netherlands: Summerville on for Malen at 46'
Sweden: Bergvall, Elanga and Zeneli introduced at 55'
Sweden: Karlstrom, Nygren and Bernhardsson departed at 56'
Netherlands: Koopmeiners and Guus Til on for Reijnders and De Jong at 60'
key_matchups: Ayari vs the Dutch box/defensive screen
Elanga vs the Netherlands right side
phases.summary: Netherlands resumed with immediate control, using repeated overloads to create easy chances and Gakpo’s quick-fire goals to stretch the game to 4-0 by 54'. Sweden briefly found a response through Elanga after a set of substitutions, but their attacking moments were isolated, while the Dutch continued to menace through Brobbey and wide pressure.

adjustments: 79' Sweden substitute Taha Ali for Ayari, changing from a midfielder to a winger
Sweden continue to seek width and pace through Elanga and Ali
Dutch defend deeper and try to compact space late on
phases.summary: This late phase was defined by Sweden chasing the game and trying to generate momentum through pace wide, with Elanga and later Taha Ali repeatedly testing the Dutch back line. The Netherlands largely managed the space well, defended with discipline in key moments, and remained dangerous enough in transition and patient buildup to finish with Summerville’s late goal.
